name: graph
description: A directed graph backed by an adjacency list, with AddEdge(a, b) to build edges and ShortestPath(from, to) ([]int, bool) that runs breadth-first search to return the fewest-edges node path (inclusive of both endpoints) or false when the target is unreachable. Standard library only, go 1.23.

maintain.request: Add a Reachable(from int) []int method that returns every node reachable from a given start node (including the start node itself), using BFS, with the result sorted in ascending order for deterministic output; return nil when the start node is not in the graph. Include table-driven tests covering cycles, isolated components, sink nodes, and a missing node.

review.bug: ShortestPath pops the frontier node from the back of the slice (queue[len(queue)-1]) instead of the front, so the traversal is last-in-first-out (depth-first) rather than a real BFS queue, and it can return a path that is not the shortest.
keywords: lifo stack instead of fifo queue
should pop from the front queue[0]
